text: Single-pixel imaging is a technique capable of imaging an object without having a direct line of sight (LOS) to the object. However, high quality imaging of the object has proven to be very difficult especially in the presence of ambient light of the environment. In this letter, we demonstrated a novel method for single-pixel imaging where the Fourier spectrum of the object is captured and an inverse Fourier transform was used to obtain the high quality image. We used phase-shifting sinusoid structured illumination for recording the spectrum of the object. We demonstrated a non-LOS imaging of the object using a single-pixel detector. Our method not only enables us to obtain high quality images of the object in ambient lighting environments, but it also achieves a compressive sampling like approach to obtain an image from below sub-Nyquist sampling rates. Finally, we have demonstrated clear images of an object behind obstacles while our camera was not in the LOS of the object even in the presence of ambient light.
explanation: The text is a coherent, field-specific abstract with plausible technical terms, but it lacks concrete experimental details or data citations, which can be a hallmark of AI-generated content.
